summary: We recommend dropping the research-engineering bootstrap because the outcomes repo is the spec home and the research scaffold belongs in a separate research repo. The tradeoff against keeping the scaffold colocated is that the spec repo gains a clearer surface and contributors do not have to skim past unrelated code. The Claude Code OTel audit is recorded in the PR description and the spec and SDK builds remain green.

claims.statement: research-engineering bootstrap directory was removed from the outcomes repo because the merged diff deletes the scaffold and the repo tree no longer contains the directory.

citations.title: outcomes main tree without the scaffold

claims.caveats: The OTel research itself is captured in the PR description for future reference.

claims.statement: spec and SDK builds remain green after the removal because the python-sdk and typescript-sdk workflows on outcomes main are passing on subsequent commits.
claims.caveats: The removal is reversible if a follow-up decision restores the research scaffold to a separate repo.

open_questions: Should the OTel research artifact move to a dedicated subagentceo research-engineering repo?
methodology_notes: Read outcomes pull 2 diff and confirmed both SDK CI workflows are green on subsequent main commits.
